search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae asthma telehealth clinical guideline 2024 PDF
site:nice.org.uk NG245 asthma monitoring review recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
The chronic workflow does not establish an acute severe attack.
Medicine names and doses were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: the exact sources include age- and test-specific recommendations, while the legacy workflow has no explicit age filter.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth asthma documentation and chronic asthma review; acute severe asthma treatment is outside the mapped content.
UAE_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth fields, with NICE used as international supporting evidence requiring UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 asthma guideline and current NICE NG245 recommendations were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer DHA issue was identified; NG245 remains current and was last updated 2025-11.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped asthma follow-up identity within DHA telehealth scope.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped asthma symptom, pattern, trigger, occupational, respiratory-examination, peak-flow, and history fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped severe-breathlessness, chest-symptom, syncope, inability-to-speak, cyanosis, altered-consciousness, and safety fields as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped peak-flow and spirometry fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped inhaler use and technique, control, response, and clinician referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Corroborates mapped symptom and objective-test fields; no duplicate item is mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped exacerbation and control-review fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped trigger and inhaler-technique discussion without medicine names or doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.

unresolved_source_gaps: The legacy workflow does not enforce the age, telehealth, chronic, or non-severe scope of the exact sources.

Hemoptysis, hospital-attendance negative, reduced oxygen wording, previous records, air entry, chest expansion, generic clinician plans, generic medication plans, fixed follow-up, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Diagnosis confirmation, severity interpretation, treatment choice, medicine dosing, emergency disposition, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
